title: podman-auto-update.service/timer: support excluding specific containers from automatic updates
body:
### Feature request description

The `podman-auto-update.timer` is a convenient way to schedule automatic container updates for quadlets using `AutoUpdate=registry`. The obvious workaround for excluding a container from automatic updates is to simply omit the auto update line from that quadlet, but this also removes it from `podman auto-update` when run manually, which is an unfortunate tradeoff. I would want `podman auto-update` to manually update my containers, while still relying on the timer to automatically update only a trusted subset of containers. Currently there is no way to express this distinction: AutoUpdate=registry is all-or-nothing across both the manual command and the timer-driven service.


### Suggest potential solution

A new quadlet key alongside AutoUpdate=registry, e.g. AutoUpdateTimer=false, that signals the service to skip that container.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Right now I simply bite the bullet and don't include AutoUpdate=registry for containers that I don't want to auto update.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
